Kimi-K3 took #1 on the Frontend Code Arena over Claude Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 Sol, and the weights go open on 27 July.It took #1 on the human-judged Frontend Code Arena over Claude Fable 5, yet loses to GPT-5.6 Sol on every static benchmark. That gap, on the largest open model ever built, is the story.
Read for Free
Press enter or click to view image in full size

The kind of output the arena was judging: a working, ray-traced black-hole simulator K3 generated as a single frontend task, from Moonshot’s own demo set. Source: Moonshot AI, Kimi-K3 technical blog.On 15 July 2026, the Frontend Code Arena crowned a new number one: Kimi-K3, from the Beijing lab Moonshot AI, at 1,679 points, ahead of Claude Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 Sol. The jump is the part that should stop you. The previous Moonshot model, K2.6, ranked 18th. K3 went from eighteenth to first in a single release and topped six of the arena’s seven frontend domains, taking second only in Gaming, behind Fable 5. Per Arena’s announcement, it is the first open-weight model to hold the top spot on that board. Moonshot ships the full weights on 27 July.
Max built a fully working Mac OS with Kimi-K3, which gives you a hint on how good this model is
